name: Smothered Pork Chops
recipeIngredient: salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan over medium-high heat, melt the butter. Stir in 1 tablespoon flour and cook, stirring constantly with a wire whisk, until lightly browned.
2. Stir in the broth and sage, mixing well. Remove from heat and set aside. This mixture will be the sauce for your chops.
3. In a bowl, mix salt and pepper with the remaining 1 tablespoon flour. Dredge the pork chops in this mixture.
4. In a large, heavy skillet over medium-high heat, add 1/4 cup lard. Add the pork chops and cook until browned. If there are too many chops to fit into the skillet at once, work in stages or use two skillets.
5. Move the browned chops to an ovenproof casserole with a cover. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
6. Melt the remaining lard in the skillet over high heat. Add onions, garlic, and celery; lower the heat to medium and cook, stirring frequently to avoid burning, until the onions are golden (8-10 minutes). Remove vegetables with a slotted spoon and place them over the chops in the casserole.
7. Pour off all lard from the skillet. Add the reserved sauce, raise the heat to high, bring to a boil, and cook for 1 minute, stirring vigorously and scraping the bottom of the skillet.
8. Pour the sauce over the chops in the casserole, cover, and bake in the preheated oven for 30 minutes.
